pattern: Binary search the smallest value whose count of multiples of either divisor reaches n, counting via inclusion-exclusion with the least common multiple, then reduce the located value modulo the fixed prime.
hints: The count of numbers up to x divisible by either divisor is monotonic in x, which invites a search over the answer's value rather than enumeration.
How many integers in [1, x] are divisible by a or b? Add the two floor counts and subtract those divisible by both, i.e. by their least common multiple.
Binary search x for the least value whose inclusion-exclusion count equals n; take the modulus only at the very end so the search arithmetic stays exact.

edge_inputs.description: first magical number is the smaller divisor
fourth magical with overlap at six
equal divisors collapse inclusion-exclusion to one term
one divisor is a multiple of the other
single count returns the minimum divisor
large n exercises the modulo reduction

stress.description: small n so the brute counter stays fast while divisors vary
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 4000)
    a = rng.randint(2, 60)
    b = rng.randint(2, 60)
    return [n, a, b]
stress.note: n kept small to bound the linear oracle; the optimal solution is unaffected
